zh.lifeSummary: 當Guillaume d'Harcourt於1090年出生在英國英格蘭萊希斯特斯爾牛頓 哈爾庫特時，他的父親Robert "Le Fort" d'Harcourt, de Cailleville, etc.56歲，母親Colette d'Argouges20歲。他和Agnes Huguette d'Amboise至少有8個兒子和8個女兒。他去世於大約1161年在法國諾門迪曼其圖爾可維爾享年72歲。

nameMeanings.name: d'Harcourt
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of the places in Eure and Calvados named Harcourt, from Old French cour(t) ‘court, farmyard’ (see Court ) with an obscure first element. This surname is very rare in France.

English: habitational name from either of two places in Shropshire named Harcourt. The one near Cleobury Mortimer gets the name from Old English heafocere ‘hawker, falconer’ + cot ‘hut, cottage’; the one near Wem has as its first element Old English hearpere (see Harper ).

History: The surname of French origin (see 1 above) is listed along with the equally rare or even extinct variants or altered forms Haraucourt, Herancourt, and Héraucourt in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors.

variants.name: Court
meanings: English: topographic or occupational name from Middle English curt, court ‘large house’ (a word of the same ultimately Latin origin as 2 below), denoting someone who lived or worked in a manor house or castle, a courtly retainer.
French: topographic name from Old French co(u)rt ‘court, farmyard’ (from Latin cohors, cors, genitive cohortis, cortis ‘court, farmyard, enclosure’).

English and French: nickname for a physically small man, or ironically for a large man, from the adjective Middle English curt, Old French co(u)rt ‘short, small’ (from Latin curtus ‘curtailed, truncated, cut short, broken off’). Compare French Lecourt .

nameMeanings.name: Guillaume
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: William
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When Guillaume d'Harcourt was born in 1090, in Newton Harcourt, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Robert "Le Fort" d'Harcourt, de Cailleville, etc., was 56 and his mother, Colette d'Argouges, was 20. He had at least 8 sons and 8 daughters with Agnes Huguette d'Amboise. He died about 1161, in Turqueville, Manche, Normandy, France, at the age of 72.

Il a eu au moins 8 fils et 8 filles avec Agnes Huguette d'Amboise. Il est décédé environ 1161, à Turqueville, Manche, Normandie, France, à l’âge de 72 ans.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Guillaume d'Harcourt è nato il nel 1090 nel Newton Harcourt, Leicestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Robert "Le Fort" d'Harcourt, de Cailleville, etc., aveva 56 anni e il suo madre, Colette d'Argouges, aveva 20 anni. Lui avuto almeno 8 figli e 8 figlie con Agnes Huguette d'Amboise. Morì circa 1161, nel Turqueville, Manche, Normandie, Francia, all'età di 72 anni.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Guillaume d'Harcourt родился в 1090 г. в местности нютон харкоурт, лисестиршир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Robert "Le Fort" d'Harcourt, de Cailleville, etc. было 56 лет, а матери, которую звали Colette d'Argouges, 20. У него было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 8 дочерей от Agnes Huguette d'Amboise. Он умер около 1161 г. в местности туркуевилл, манч, норманди, Франция в возрасте 72 лет.
